findings.where: Paragraph under “The problem with tangent lines”
findings.issue: Over-generalized claim that a tangent line touches the curve at only one point.
findings.evidence: The draft says: “a tangent line touches the curve at only one point.” Tangent lines can intersect the curve elsewhere, and can even cross the curve at the point of tangency.
findings.suggestion: Avoid the one-point-contact characterization. Define the tangent locally via the limiting slope of secant lines at P.

findings.issue: The direction brief mandates a secant-to-tangent convergence figure, but the draft provides only a captioned empty figure element.
findings.evidence: `<figure class="figure" data-fig="secant-to-tangent">` contains only `<figcaption>... Secant lines ... converging ...</figcaption>` and no visible diagram content such as image, SVG, canvas, or plotted markup.
findings.suggestion: Add an actual visual convergence diagram, or ensure this `data-fig` hook is resolved by the kit into a visible figure.

summary: Two blocking findings: one mathematical over-generalization and one missing brief-mandated visual. The limit definition, h-form, f'(a) as a point derivative, worked examples, exercises, and sqrt(4.01) bookend otherwise align with the seed and brief.
